name: Hawaiian Community Assets, Inc. - Wailuku Branch
description_short: Hawaiian Community Assets is a nonprofit offering free financial education, grants, and loans to help Hawaii residents build wealth and access affordable housing across four island locations.
description_long:
Hawaiian Community Assets (HCA) is a community development organization that has been serving Hawaii's residents since 2000, celebrating 25 years of operations. The organization operates four offices across the Hawaiian Islands—located in Līhuʻe (Kaua'i), Hilo (Hawai'i), Honolulu (O'ahu), and Wailuku (Maui)—with a mission focused on strengthening communities through housing and financial empowerment.

HCA offers comprehensive financial services including free financial education classes, training courses, and workshops. They provide grants and loans to support affordable housing initiatives and partner with Hawaii Community Lending (HCL), their sister nonprofit CDFI organization, to offer mortgage financing assistance and additional lending products. The organization has demonstrated significant community impact, having served 22,287 Hawaii residents with financial education and helped 7,346 children and adults access affordable homes, while facilitating access to $165+ million in affordable housing support.

What distinguishes Hawaiian Community Assets is their deep community rootedness and multi-island presence across Hawaii, their specific focus on Hawaiian Community Lands Financial Assessments, and specialized programs like Ua Hale Aʻela (rent-to-own housing). They also demonstrate crisis responsiveness, with documented programs assisting those affected by the Lahaina Wildfires. As a free-help organization, all financial education workshops are offered at no cost to residents.

HCA is best suited for Hawaii residents seeking free financial education, first-time homebuyers interested in affordable housing programs, and those affected by recent natural disasters. The primary limitation is geographic restriction—services are only available in Hawaii through their four offices. The website provides limited detailed information about specific loan terms, interest rates, or eligibility requirements, requiring potential clients to contact offices directly.
pros: All financial education classes, training courses, and workshops are completely free
Multi-island presence with four offices serving Kaua'i, Hawai'i, O'ahu, and Maui
Nonprofit 501(c)3 organization with 25 years of documented community impact
Partnership with Hawaii Community Lending provides access to grants, loans, and mortgage financing
Specialized programs including Hawaiian Home Lands Financial Assessments and Ua Hale Aʻela rent-to-own options
Demonstrated crisis response with programs for Lahaina Wildfire-affected residents
Accessible contact method via phone/text at single unified number (808) 587-7886
cons: Services limited exclusively to Hawaii residents—no mainland or out-of-state support
Website lacks specific information about loan terms, APR rates, or detailed eligibility criteria
Limited online service delivery—appears to require in-person office visits for many programs
No information about wait times, application processing timelines, or loan approval rates
Website does not clearly explain differences between HCA services and sister organization HCL services
best_for: Hawaii residents seeking free financial education and workshops to improve money management
First-time homebuyers interested in affordable housing programs and mortgage assistance
Individuals looking to purchase or build on Hawaiian Home Lands
Disaster-affected residents (such as Lahaina Wildfire survivors) seeking housing and financial recovery support
diagnosis: Hawaiian Community Assets is best for Hawaii-based residents seeking free financial education and affordable housing programs with nonprofit support. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to four Hawaiian islands and the need to contact offices directly for specific loan details, as the website provides limited information about rates, terms, and detailed eligibility requirements.
services: Free financial education classes and workshops
Loans for housing and financial needs
Mortgage financing assistance (through partner Hawaii Community Lending)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Hawaiian Community Assets: nonprofit offering free financial education, grants, and loans for wealth-building and affordable housing across Hawaii.
